title: Hide the base queries from visualization

blocks.content:
When using SQL expressions, the base queries provide input data but you typically don't want them rendered in the panel. Hiding a query prevents it from appearing in the visualization while keeping it available for expressions to reference.

To hide the base queries, complete the following steps:
On query row **A**, click the **eye icon** (Hide response) in the row header to hide query A from the visualization.
On query row **B**, click the **eye icon** to hide query B as well.

Both query rows now show a crossed-out eye icon and the panel preview is empty. The queries still run and return data, but their results are hidden from the visualization. Only the SQL expression output (which you add next) will be visible.

In the next milestone, you add a SQL expression.
